mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance or negative mean returns during periods of falling federal funds rates over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment rate rises by over 0.3 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate profit margins and generates negative equity returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production growth coincides with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 50% of late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the consumer price index below 1.5 percent year-over-year removes inflationary pressure and allows markets to price in further monetary accommodation, yielding positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of months where CPI year-over-year growth drops below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment signals labor market slack and wage stagnation, depressing consumer spending forecasts and producing negative equity returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Periods of simultaneous PAYEMS growth and UNRATE increases yield positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, creating a persistent positive drift in equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 quarterly growth fails to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable inflation confirms capacity utilization improvements that boost profit margins and trigger immediate positive equity repricing during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth alongside stable CPI yields negative 5-day forward returns in over 55% of early-cycle expansion observations.
